item.title: The case for being exclusive at work
item.description: Most leaders understand their message needs to define exactly who their work is for. Fewer realize that it should also define who it’s not for. Fewer ...

item.title: Change doesn’t fail by itself. It fails because people resist it
item.description: Change often fails and that rarely has anything to do with whether the concept is a good one or not. As Howard Aiken famously put it, “Don’t worry abo...

item.title: I founded 1-800-Flowers.com 50 years ago. This is the secret to business success
item.description: Fifty years is a long time for any company to stay in business. About 20% fail in their first year. By year five, roughly half are gone. By the end of...

item.title: How hesitation is a fundamental brain feature, according to neuroscientists
item.description: At the Winter Olympics, skiers, bobsledders, speedskaters, and many other athletes all have to master one critical moment: when to start. That split s...

item.title: Brendan Fraser on resilience at work in your 50s: ‘The silences in a career can be deafening’
